lastSession.summary: Fixed critical production bug: dashboard losing connection to master-server. Root cause was nginx caching DNS resolution at startup - when master-server restarted with new IP, nginx tried old IP getting Connection refused. Fixed by configuring nginx with Docker DNS resolver (127.0.0.11) and variable-based upstream pattern to force runtime DNS resolution. Deployed fix and verified connection stability.

pendingWork: Monitor production for any other connection issues
Test dashboard auto-refresh functionality works correctly now

recentDecisions.what: Configured nginx to use Docker's DNS resolver with variable-based upstream for dynamic IP resolution
recentDecisions.why: nginx's default behavior of caching DNS at startup is incompatible with Docker's dynamic IP allocation on container restarts

recentDecisions.what: Added persistent Docker volumes for ML models and training data to prevent data loss on container restarts
recentDecisions.why: MODEL_STORAGE_PATH and TRAINING_DATA_PATH had no volumes mounted - any ML models or training signals would be lost when containers restart

recentDecisions.what: Added log rotation configuration to all production Docker containers to prevent disk space exhaustion
recentDecisions.why: No log rotation configured - logs could grow indefinitely and fill disk over time

recentDecisions.what: Added .env.docker.prod to gitignore to prevent accidental commit of production secrets
recentDecisions.why: File contains POSTGRES_PASSWORD, API keys, JWT_SECRET, and Cloudflare tunnel token - critical security risk if committed to git

recentDecisions.what: Comprehensive production configuration audit completed - identified critical and high-risk issues
recentDecisions.why: Recent production issues revealed incomplete configuration. Systematic analysis needed to prevent future incidents.

recentProblems.description: Dashboard losing connection to master-server repeatedly after being online for a few minutes
Fixed nginx DNS caching issue causing dashboard disconnections after master-server restarts

recentLessons: nginx caches DNS resolution at startup by default - critical issue in Docker where containers can get new IPs on restart
Production environment audits must check for volume persistence - ephemeral containers need explicit volumes for all critical data paths
Docker log rotation is not enabled by default - must be explicitly configured to prevent production disk space issues
